title: Multiple access point negotiation for transmission opportunity sharing
abstract: A first access point (AP) in a wireless network, the first AP comprising memory and a processor coupled to the memory, the processor configured to: transmit, to a plurality of APs including a second AP, a request frame requesting a transmission opportunity (TXOP) sharing, wherein the request frame comprises a set of parameters for the TXOP sharing, and receive, from the second AP, a response frame indicating the second AP accepts a request for the TXOP sharing.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A first access point (AP) in a wireless network, the first AP comprising: memory; and a processor coupled to the memory, the processor configured to: transmit, to a plurality of APs including a second AP, a request frame requesting a transmission opportunity (TXOP) sharing, wherein the request frame comprises a set of parameters for the TXOP sharing, and receive, from the second AP, a response frame indicating that the second AP accepts a request for the TXOP sharing. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to: prior to transmitting the request frame, transmit, to the plurality of APs, an announcement frame indicating an intention to initiate a multi-AP coordination; and receive, from the second AP, a preparedness frame indicating capabilities to participate in the multi-AP coordination. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to: receive, from a third AP included in the plurality of APs, a response frame indicating that the third AP rejects the request for the TXOP sharing. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the processor is further configured to: receive, from a third AP included in the plurality of APs, a response frame including an alternative set of parameters for the TXOP sharing; and transmit, to the third AP, another request frame including the alternative set of parameters so that the third AP participates in the TXOP sharing. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the set of parameters specify rules regarding sharing a TXOP with other stations (STAs). The first AP of claim 1, wherein the set of parameters includes peer-to-peer (P2P) information related to the P2P stations (STAs) in a Basic Service Set (BSS) managed by the first AP. The first AP of claim 6, wherein the set of parameters includes P2P information related to the P2P STAs in a BSS managed by the second AP. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the set of parameters includes timing information on a portion of a TXOP that is allocated to the second AP. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the set of parameters includes channel information indicating a set of channels that the second AP is allowed to use. The first AP of claim 1, wherein the set of parameters includes operating class information indicating a set of operating classes that the second AP is allowed to use. A first access point (AP) that serves as a central controller to coordinate transmission opportunity (TXOP) sharing in a wireless network, the first AP comprising: memory; and a processor coupled to the memory, the processor configured to: receive, from a second AP, a request frame requesting a transmission opportunity (TXOP) sharing; transmit, to the second AP, a response frame indicating that the second AP accepts a request for the TXOP sharing; transmit, to a plurality of APs including a third AP, a frame including  a set of parameters for the TXOP sharing; and receive an acknowledgement frame from the third AP. The first AP of claim 11, wherein the processor is further configured to: prior to receiving the request frame, transmit, to the plurality of APs, an announcement frame indicating an intention to initiate a multi-AP coordination; and receive, from the third AP, a preparedness frame indicating capabilities to participate in the multi-AP coordination. The first AP of claim 11, wherein the processor is further configured to: receive, from a fourth AP, a response frame indicating that the fourth AP rejects the request for the TXOP sharing. The first AP of claim 11, wherein the processor is further configured to: receive, from a fourth AP included in the plurality of APs, a response frame including an alternative set of parameters for the TXOP sharing; and transmit, to the fourth AP, another request frame including the alternative set of parameters so that the fourth AP participates in the TXOP sharing. The first AP of claim 11, wherein the set of parameters specify rules regarding sharing a TXOP with other stations (STAs).
